brick: The Form-native buffer bridge runs ON THE ANDROID PHONE's fkwu, and the binary-safe ranged file read (read_file_slice_bytes, opcode 127) crosses four-way AND on-device. Observed by implementing the android fkwu lane and adjusting the one missing piece a real on-device run surfaced.
author: Sema (Opus 4.8, real-model-e2e worktree)

what_runs.fkwu_on_android: the Form-emitted universal walker (fkc-emit-universal), NDK-cross-compiled to an aarch64 ELF, pushed to a real Galaxy S23 Ultra and run standalone on a flattened band table — NO Go/Rust/TS/python in the runtime (Go is the build-time flattener only; NDK clang is the c-bootstrap).
what_runs.scripts/android_fkwu_band_receipt.sh: emit fkwu C -> NDK aarch64 compile -> adb push -> run on device -> verdict. The android row of the standard receipt.

on_device_receipts.device: SM-S918U1 (Galaxy S23 Ultra), Android, arm64-v8a, adb 192.168.1.223:5555
on_device_receipts.field-identity: 511 on the phone's fkwu (== four-way verdict)
on_device_receipts.safetensors-decode: 15 on the phone's fkwu (the buffer-bridge F16 decode core)
on_device_receipts.read-file-slice-bytes: 7 on the phone's fkwu (write via file_append_bytes -> binary-exact read via read_file_slice_bytes opcode 127 -> fd-f16 decode)

the_adjust.observed: On the phone, field-identity (511) and safetensors-decode (15) ran, but read-file-slice-bytes FAILED to flatten: 'trivialValue composite'. Isolated to read_file_slice_bytes (opcode 127) alone — file_append_bytes/str_concat/sum all flattened fine.
the_adjust.root_cause: read_file_slice_bytes was wired into the native table (form-flatten.fk), the host-io class, and the WALKER C handler (t==127), but NOT the flattener's per-opcode dispatch in fkc-flat. read_file_slice (62) has an explicit (fkc-tri2 62 ...); opcode 127 fell through to the binary path and crashed on the 3-arg composite.
the_adjust.fix: added (if (eq (fk-tag n) 127) (fkc-tri2 127 (fkc-flat (ms-child (fk-body n) 0) acc) n) ...) beside the 62 dispatch in hati-os-kernel-emit.fk, balanced the chain. Opcode 127 now flattens, crosses four-way, and runs on android.

what_changed: form-kernel-go/main.go, form-kernel-rust/src/main.rs, form-kernel-ts/src/kernel.ts: read_file_slice_bytes native (binary-safe ranged read: byte LIST, not a UTF-8 string that corrupts bytes >= 0x80)
form-stdlib/form-flatten.fk: read_file_slice_bytes native-table row (opcode 127) + host-io classification
form-stdlib/hati-os-kernel-emit.fk: walker C handler (t==127, builds a byte cons-list) + the fkc-flat per-opcode dispatch (the piece the on-device run surfaced)
form-stdlib/safetensors.fk + safetensors-decode.fk: the Form-native safetensors loader (header parse + fd-f16 decode + per-row binary reads)
scripts/android_fkwu_band_receipt.sh: the on-device fkwu receipt lane
honest_floor: Toolchain status on the android lane: c-bootstrap is NDK-clang today (the standard receipt's c-bootstrap row stays pending until form-elf emits the fkwu ELF bytes directly, dropping clang); Go is the build-time flattener (table). RUNTIME on the phone is toolchain-free (the fkwu ELF links bionic only). The buffer-bridge READ at real model scale on android (heap sizing for large tensors) is the next observe-and-adjust.
